summary: Project has no detected systems or open drift. Guardrails/context are present but architecture state is still sparse.

north_star.text: Keep Tack as an offline-first architecture guardrail and deterministic handoff engine.
Reduce hallucination risk by enforcing file-grounded state and explicit constraints.
Preserve continuity across agents through structured context docs and canonical handoff JSON.
Keep power mode and conversational mode on the same deterministic core functions.

open_questions.text: Should `tack check-in` ship in v1 or stay deferred behind MCP resources?
Where should domain tags be defined in spec schema without breaking v1 compatibility?
What exact compaction thresholds should trigger before handoff generation?
Should resolved assumptions auto-convert into dated decisions during compaction?

assumptions.text: Users run Tack from the target project root so `/.tack/` aligns to intended scope.
Detectors remain deterministic and file-system based (no external APIs).
Handoff consumers prioritize JSON output as canonical over markdown formatting.
Conversational mode will call existing deterministic functions instead of writing direct engine outputs.
LLM usage remains optional; core flows work without API keys.
Watch mode occupies one terminal and users run one-shot commands in a second terminal.

recent_decisions.reasoning: power mode now, conversational mode later, both on same deterministic engine

recent_decisions.decision: Bare `tack` defaults to init if `.tack/` missing, otherwise watch
Integrity repair runs automatically when spec exists but support files are missing
[deferred] Migrate detectors from TS modules to YAML registry
recent_decisions.reasoning: current TS detectors are fine for v1; revisit for new ecosystems and community rules

recent_decisions.decision: Framework and auth detectors moved to YAML (src/detectors/rules/*.yaml + yamlRunner)
recent_decisions.reasoning: data-driven rules, binary detection, getRulesDir for dev and dist; database/payments/jobs/etc remain TS

next_steps.text: Review 7 changed file(s) for spec compliance
